steps.title: Identify what the student wants to emphasize
steps.content:
The goal is to emphasize Mauritania’s global rank — specifically how Mauritania compares to other countries worldwide in its under-fifteen population. The notes state Mauritania is thirty-fourth in the world for that age group. Any correct answer must include that global ranking.
  • Choice A — With 39.9 percent of its population under fifteen years of age, Mauritania has the thirty-fourth highest population for that age range in the world.: Includes both the percentage and the global rank ("thirty-fourth highest...in the world"), directly fulfilling the goal. ✓
  • Choice B — Africa's high population of young people is due in part to the high percentage of young people in Mauritania.: Focuses on Africa broadly, not on Mauritania’s global ranking. ✗
  • Choice C — Making up roughly 40 percent of the continent's total population, Africa's under-fifteen population offers “an opportunity for the continent's growth,” according to the UN.: Shifts to Africa as a whole and the UN quote; omits Mauritania’s world ranking entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — "Only if these new generations are fully empowered to realise their best potential," says the UN, will Africa's high percentage of young people lead to the continent's growth.: Quotes the UN about empowerment; says nothing about Mauritania’s rank. ✗

steps.title: Select the choice that delivers the global rank
steps.content: Only Choice A — With 39.9 percent of its population under fifteen years of age, Mauritania has the thirty-fourth highest population for that age range in the world. mentions Mauritania’s position relative to other countries worldwide ("thirty-fourth highest...in the world"). The remaining choices redirect attention to Africa as a continent or to UN commentary, neither of which emphasizes Mauritania’s global rank. That matches Choice A — With 39.9 percent of its population under fifteen years of age, Mauritania has the thirty-fourth highest population for that age range in the world..
